description: Constant-time analysis benchmark corpus. Items labeled clean (designed CT, expect 0 errors after secret-aware filtering), vulnerable (real timing leak, expect detection), or limitation_no_signal (real leak that instruction-level analysis cannot detect; documents an analyzer limitation).

items.label: clean
items.notes: Canonical CT primitives: value_barrier_w, constant_time_msb_w, constant_time_lt_w, constant_time_eq_w, constant_time_select_w. Bitwise/shift only; no branches, no division, no table indexing on secrets. value_barrier_w uses an inline asm clobber to defeat compiler simplification.

items.label: clean
items.notes: CRYPTO_memcmp loops len times unconditionally, accumulating XOR via OR. Loop bound `len` is PUBLIC. Final shift+sub is bit-twiddling, not a branch.

items.source: OpenSSL crypto/cryptlib.c CRYPTO_memcmp (1.1.1-stable branch; removed from master cryptlib.c)
items.label: clean
items.notes: OpenSSL's textbook CT byte comparator. Pointers are volatile to prevent compiler-inserted early exits.

items.label: clean
items.notes: sodium_memcmp + sodium_is_zero. volatile-qualified pointers and accumulator block compiler-inserted early exits. Final return uses (d-1)>>8 bit collapse.

items.label: clean
items.notes: mbedtls_ct_memcmp plus mbedtls_ct_uint_eq / mbedtls_ct_uint_if_else_0. Bitwise-mask combinations rather than branches. The unrolled 4-byte path was stripped because it requires MBEDTLS_EFFICIENT_UNALIGNED_VOLATILE_ACCESS asm helpers.

items.label: clean
items.notes: ChaCha20 is intentionally CT: ARX only; no branches/division/table-lookup on secrets. Loop counters and `len` are PUBLIC (any branch on `len` is a false positive).

items.label: clean
items.notes: BoringSSL HChaCha20. The for-loop bound (20) is a public constant. memcpy with sizeof(...) is also public-sized.

items.label: clean
items.notes: 20-round ChaCha20 block; identical CT properties to the reference. No data-dependent branches, divisions, or table accesses.

items.label: clean
items.notes: Conditional-swap and conditional-move primitives for the X25519 ladder. `b = 0 - b` converts a 0/1 bit to a bit-mask; the result is used only in AND/XOR. Loop bound FE_NUM_LIMBS is public.

items.label: clean
items.notes: 5-limb Curve25519 field-element multiply. Branchless reduction with shift+mask; no DIV; no secret-dependent control flow.

items.label: vulnerable

ground_truth_violations.description: secret_coef / GAMMA2 emits idiv on secret dividend
secret % 3329 emits idiv on secret dividend

items.notes: Original ML-KEM vulnerability that prompted the KyberSlash CVEs. The `public_block_count` function (line 36) uses / on a public length and is a deliberate FALSE-POSITIVE test.

items.label: vulnerable

ground_truth_violations.description: for-loop bound is the secret padding_len
variable-time memcmp on the MAC bytes

items.notes: CBC padding oracle. The pre-loop bound check `if (padding_len > len - 1)` on line 19 IS public (false positive if flagged).

items.label: vulnerable

ground_truth_violations.description: branch on a bit of secret exponent
(base * base) % mod is in the unconditional path but operates on secret-derived base
Montgomery ladder still uses % mod on secret-derived r0, r1 - real timing leak even without the secret branch

items.notes: Classic naive modexp + Montgomery ladder.  Both functions leak: naive via secret branching AND modulo, the ladder via the modulo alone.  Truly constant-time modexp requires Montgomery multiplication or Barrett reduction (no idiv).

items.label: vulnerable

ground_truth_violations.description: memcmp early-exits on first differing byte
branch on memcmp result decides accept/reject
strcmp on token has the same early-exit leak

items.notes: Standard authentication-bypass anti-pattern. memcmp/strcmp do not promise constant-time semantics.

items.label: limitation_no_signal
items.notes: Cache-timing leak via secret-indexed table lookup. NOT detectable by an instruction-level analyzer (no DIV, no JCC). Included to document a known limitation. Expect 0 findings; the leak is real but invisible to this class of tool.

fetch_failures: openssl master crypto/cryptlib.c — CRYPTO_memcmp moved out of cryptlib.c on master; fell back to OpenSSL_1_1_1-stable branch
BoringSSL .c paths — repo migrated to .cc (C++); fetched .cc and extracted into .c snippets
